title: T-016 — API: BOM Generator batch endpoint (POST /api/technical/bom-generator)
prompt:
# T-016 — API: BOM Generator batch endpoint (POST /api/technical/bom-generator)

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §7.3, §7.6
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
BOM Generator endpoint enqueuing async XLSX job, V-TEC-15 enforcement.

Use existing outbox pattern (00-FOUNDATION §10). Worker generates BOM_FG<code>.xlsx per FG when output_mode='per_fg', or BOM_Batch_<date>.xlsx with N sheets when 'single_batch'. Audit row action='bom_batch_generate'. Skip FGs not in Status_Overall='Complete'. Do not create WIP items here; WIP/intermediate records are generated by NPD Builder before Technical handoff.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- app/api/technical/bom-generator/route.ts [create]
- lib/technical/bom/generator.ts [create]
- lib/technical/bom/generator-worker.ts [create]
- tests/api/bom-generator.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- UI modal (T-041)
- D365 Builder (01-NPD)

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given 3 FGs of which 2 are Status_Overall='Complete', when POST scope='all_complete', then expected_count=2 and only 2 are queued.
2. Given output_mode='per_fg' with 2 FGs, when worker runs, then 2 distinct XLSX URLs are produced.
3. Given user without technical.bom.generate_batch, when POST runs, then 403.
4. Given POST runs, when audit_log queried, then action='bom_batch_generate' row exists.

## Test strategy
- RED tests for V-TEC-15 filter + RBAC + output mode.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/api/bom-generator.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not generate XLSX synchronously inside the request (must be outbox-async).
- Do not include private_jsonb in XLSX output.
- Do not treat D365 Builder as the canonical creator of product/BOM/WIP; Monopilot/NPD Builder handoff is canonical.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
